name: backend-atomic-commit
description: Pedantic backend pre-commit and atomic commit Skill for Django/Optimo-style repos. Enforces local AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md, pre-commit hooks, .security/* helpers, and Monty’s backend engineering taste – with no AI signatures in commit messages.
capabilities: Run Ruff formatting and linting fixes on changed Python files
Execute ty type checking scoped to modified files only
Run Django system checks and targeted pytest subsets
Enforce local AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md repository rules
Detect and fix local imports, missing type hints, and logging patterns
Generate ticket-prefixed commit messages without AI signatures
author: DiversioTeam
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing instructions for an AI agent to run local development tools. All 275 static findings are false positives. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections are markdown code block syntax, not actual shell commands. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings trigger on markdown headings containing 'MD5' in context of banning insecure patterns. The '1Password data' and 'Environment file access' findings are documentation recommending AGAINST staging secrets. No executable code exists in this skill.
valueStatement: Backend code often fails CI quality gates and contains non-atomic commits. This skill runs Ruff, ty, Django checks, and security scripts to fix issues and enforce commit standards without AI signatures.
userTitle: Enforce backend coding standards and atomic commits
limitations: Requires Django project structure with manage.py
Needs uv or local tool wrappers in .bin/ directory
Does not modify migration files or schema changes automatically
Falls back to generic Python checks for non-Django projects

useCases.title: Auto-fix code before commit
useCases.description: Run pre-commit mode to fix formatting, imports, type hints, and logging patterns in staged files before CI runs

useCases.title: Enforce commit atomicity
useCases.description: Verify staged changes represent one logical change and all quality gates pass before creating a commit

useCases.title: Standardize backend tooling
useCases.description: Apply consistent Ruff, ty, and Django checks across all backend contributions with clear blocking issues

promptTemplates.title: Fix pre-commit issues
promptTemplates.prompt: Run /backend-atomic-commit:pre-commit on this repo and fix all files in git status to obey AGENTS.md and pre-commit hooks

promptTemplates.title: Prepare atomic commit
promptTemplates.prompt: Use /backend-atomic-commit:atomic-commit to prepare an atomic commit for staged changes in backend/

promptTemplates.title: Strict mode fix
promptTemplates.prompt: Run /backend-atomic-commit:pre-commit in strict mode: eliminate local imports, fix type hints, clean debug statements

promptTemplates.title: Verify commit readiness
promptTemplates.prompt: Run /backend-atomic-commit:atomic-commit --auto to enforce structured logging, verify tests, and report commit readiness

bestPractices: Run pre-commit mode before staging files to catch issues early and avoid CI failures
Always address [BLOCKING] items before attempting atomic commits
Use scoped ty check to catch type errors without triggering baseline errors in unmodified files
antiPatterns: Running atomic-commit mode without reviewing the proposed commit message first
Ignoring [SHOULD_FIX] items that accumulate over multiple commits
Using local imports to avoid cyclic import problems instead of refactoring

faq.question: What tools does this skill require?
faq.answer: Requires uv, Ruff, ty type checker, and Django management commands. Looks for .bin/ wrappers first with uv run fallback.

faq.question: Does this work with non-Django Python projects?
faq.answer: Falls back to generic Python pre-commit behavior if no manage.py or Django-style AGENTS.md is detected.

faq.question: How does it handle pre-existing code issues?
faq.answer: Resolves ALL issues in files you modify, not just new ones. CI runs checks on modified files only.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Runs locally on your machine using only local tools. No data is sent anywhere. Git operations stay in your repo.

faq.question: Why are my commits showing AI signatures?
faq.answer: This skill explicitly prevents AI signatures. Check your AI tool settings or other plugins that may add them.

faq.question: How is this different from pre-commit hooks?
faq.answer: Runs the same checks as your hooks but also auto-fixes issues and enforces atomic commit structure.
